facts: Pluto's orbit is highly elliptical, causing huge temperature swings.
When Pluto is farthest from the Sun, temperatures can plunge below -240°C (-400°F).
At these extreme cold temperatures, Pluto's thin nitrogen atmosphere FREEZES solid and falls onto its surface like snow.
idea.payoff: So, for decades at a time, Pluto literally loses its atmosphere as it freezes and reforms when its orbit brings it closer to the Sun!
idea.title: Pluto's Vanishing Act! 👻🧊

scenes.visual: A vibrant, stylized illustration of Pluto with a visible thin atmosphere. The atmosphere quickly fades away, revealing a barren surface, then reappears subtly.

scenes.text: Pluto's orbit is highly elliptical, causing HUGE temp swings.
scenes.visual: An animation showing a highly elliptical orbit path around a glowing, distant sun. A small Pluto icon moves along the path, with labels 'FARTHEST (COLD)' and 'CLOSEST (WARM)' appearing.

scenes.text: Farthest from Sun, temps PLUNGE below -240°C (-400°F).
scenes.visual: A close-up view of a desolate, icy Pluto surface. A digital thermometer graphic quickly drops from above zero to below -240°C, showing extreme cold.

scenes.text: Its thin N₂ atmosphere FREEZES solid & falls like snow.
scenes.visual: Animation depicting Pluto's surface. Visible gas molecules (N₂) in a thin layer begin to condense and fall as tiny, glowing snowflakes onto the icy landscape.

scenes.text: Pluto literally loses its atmosphere for DECADES, then it reforms when closer to the Sun!
scenes.visual: A continuous loop animation of Pluto, showing its atmosphere slowly fading out completely, leaving a bare planet, and then slowly reforming again, creating a cycle. Stars twinkle in the background.
